title: A Shared IPTC Topic Space for Cross-Source Topic Modelling
abstract: arXiv:2606.26845v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Comparing topic attention across different media is hindered by a fundamental modelling problem: topic models fitted separately to each corpus produce corpus-specific topic spaces that cannot be aligned directly. This paper presents a reproducible framework that places corpora in a single shared topic space defined by a taxonomy. Discovered topics are obtained with guided BERTopic, scored against the ninety-four IPTC Media Topics' taxonomy topics (level-1) through weighted keyword and target centroids, and then collapsed upward to seventeen IPTC parent topics by a maximum-similarity rule. The framework was developed and selected on a controlled New York Times 2011 corpus through a narrowing sequence: a broad model screen, a focused mapping refinement, a strict finalist comparison, a target-construction ablation, and a threshold calibration. In this corpus, the guided family retained substantially stronger mapped coverage than a zero-shot

Title: A Shared IPTC Topic Space for Cross-Source Topic Modelling

translated_title: 用于跨源主题建模的共享IPTC主题空间
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26845v1 公告类型：新  摘要：比较不同媒体间的主题关注度受到一个基本建模问题的阻碍：分别对每个语料库拟合的主题模型会产生无法直接对齐的语料库特定主题空间。本文提出了一个可复现的框架，该框架将语料库置于由分类法定义的单一共享主题空间中。通过引导式BERTopic获得发现的主题，通过加权关键词和目标质心，对照94个IPTC媒体主题分类法主题（第一级）进行评分，然后通过最大相似性规则向上合并为17个IPTC父主题。该框架是在受控的《纽约时报》2011年语料库上通过一系列逐步缩小的过程开发并选定的：包括广泛模型筛选、聚焦映射优化、严格决赛比较、目标构建消融实验以及阈值校准。在该语料库中，引导式家族相比零样本方法保持了显著更强的映射覆盖。
tldr: 本文提出一个基于IPTC分类法的可复现框架，通过引导式BERTopic将不同语料库映射到共享主题空间，显著提升跨源主题建模的对齐能力。
en_tdlr: This paper presents a reproducible framework that maps different corpora into a shared IPTC-based topic space via guided BERTopic, significantly improving alignment for cross-source topic modeling.
